## Biographical sketch

Miel Hostens’ long‑term research vision addresses one of the defining challenges of this century: sustaining animal‑protein production for a global population approaching 10–11 billion while simultaneously reducing environmental impact and safeguarding animal health, welfare, and food safety. His work is rooted in **precision dairy and veterinary science**, with a strong commitment to renewing society’s “license to produce” through sustainable, data‑driven livestock systems aligned with the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals.

Over the past two decades, Hostens has been at the forefront of **precision livestock farming (PLF)**, integrating real‑time sensing technologies that monitor production, behavior, health, and environmental emissions. He has led and contributed to large national and international data initiatives, including **dairydatawarehouse.com** and **MmmooOgle.com**, which aggregate heterogeneous, real‑world dairy data across farms, technologies, and countries. These platforms exemplify his approach to leveraging existing data infrastructures to move beyond isolated experiments toward scalable, evidence‑based insights for both science and practice.

A central challenge limiting the impact of PLF is that data remain **horizontally scattered** across farms, vendors, research institutes, and public–private partnerships, constrained by interoperability gaps, data quality issues, and unresolved questions of ownership, privacy, and security. Hostens’ research program directly addresses these barriers by developing **methodological frameworks that bring computation to the data rather than centralizing the data itself**. His vision centers on the creation of a **privacy‑preserving federated testbed** that combines (1) **ontologies** to harmonize and map heterogeneous data sources, (2) **digital twins** of cows and farms for hypothesis testing, simulation, and training, and (3) **federated learning** to train statistical, machine‑learning, and AI models across distributed data sources without transferring raw data.

To ensure practical adoption, this framework is extended with **fine‑tuned large language models (LLMs)** and **retrieval‑augmented generation (RAG)**, enabling farmers, veterinarians, and policymakers to interact with complex digital twins through natural language while retaining local context, privacy, and scientific grounding. By integrating historical experimental data, real‑time sensor streams, and genomic information, the program aims to generate precision phenotypes, support causal inference, enable simulation‑based decision making, and ultimately translate advanced analytics into actionable guidance at farm and industry levels.

## Motivational insights

Understanding what somebody does often starts by understanding one's motivational insights. [You can read my motivational insights](/files/pdfs/miel-hostens-insights.pdf) (created in 2022, I might have changed a little bit meanwhile :). Most of the ideas in the report can be found in the absolutely must-read "[Surrounded by Idiots](https://www.surroundedbyidiots.com/en/books/surrounded-by-idiots/)" by Thomas Erikson. His book and my own motivational insights have been a personal and academic game changer for me.
